docs: https://docs.customerly.io (collection index) + https://customerlypublic.docs.apiary.io/ (full endpoint reference blueprint, fetched 2026-07-04)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every endpoint documented in the Customerly Apiary API blueprint (customerlypublic.docs.apiary.io) is accounted for below, XOR'd between covered_by (streams/writes this bundle now implements) and excluded (with a real closed-vocabulary category+reason). Knowledge-base content management (collections/articles/writers) is a distinct product surface (help-center authoring, not contact/CRM data) and is excluded out_of_scope. The previously-invented '/v1/conversations', '/v1/attributes', '/v1/events' entries did not correspond to any documented endpoint in the public blueprint and have been removed rather than carried forward as fictional surface.

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: response body is {"data": ["tag1", "tag2", ...]} -- a bare JSON array of scalar strings, not objects; connsdk.RecordsAt only keeps array elements that decode as a JSON object, so a scalar-valued array silently yields zero records, and records.keyed_object explodes an object's VALUES (which must themselves be objects), not a flat array of strings. No declarative primitive fans a bare scalar array into one record per element (same gap class as ip2whois's nameservers deviation, docs/migration/conventions.md ledger item 12); see docs.md Known limits

ENGINE_GAP: the documented request body wraps records in a named singleton/bulk array ("users": [{...}]), and the dialect's write body construction (body_type json/form/none, body_fields allow-list) has no primitive to nest a record inside a named array wrapper -- see docs.md Known limits
ENGINE_GAP: same array-wrap body shape as create_user ("leads": [{...}]); no declarative primitive nests a record inside a named array wrapper -- see docs.md Known limits

single-record lookup by email over the same User resource the users/list stream already paginates in full; no bulk/filter parameter makes this a distinct catalog stream, and the dialect has no per-record detail-fetch mechanism outside fan_out (which needs a parent id list, not a caller-supplied email)
single-record lookup by user_id over the same User resource the users/list stream already paginates in full
single-record lookup by email over the same Lead resource the leads/list stream already paginates in full

irreversibly deletes a user's full conversation/survey/campaign history by email; delete_user (by user_id) already covers the same destructive delete-by-identifier action and writes.json actions are keyed one per action name, so a second email-keyed delete_user variant would collide on the same name
help-center content authoring (create a KB collection), a distinct product surface from contact/CRM data; not a syncable object stream or a CRM mutation this connector's capability scope targets
help-center KB collection listing; distinct product surface from contact/CRM data, out of scope for this connector
help-center KB single-collection fetch; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB collection delete; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB collection edit; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB article listing; distinct product surface from contact/CRM data, out of scope
help-center KB article authoring; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB single-article fetch; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB article delete; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB article edit; distinct product surface, out of scope
help-center KB writer/author roster listing; distinct product surface, out of scope
